title: Head of Service Repairs and Material Handling
description:
The Head of Service Repairs and Material Handling is a senior leadership role within the frontline organization, responsible for enabling consistent, efficient, and customer‑focused service repairs. This position oversees repair offering and commercialization, repair tendering, repair pricing, materials and spare parts support, inspection report analysis, and repair coordination teams, while ensuring strong collaboration with service sales, service operations, customer service, regional leadership, and other commercial stakeholders. The role reports to the Service Director, Middle East, Africa, and Türkiye .

**Key responsibilities for this** **position:**

* Lead the centralized Service Repairs team, covering repair offering and commercialization, repair pricing, repair tendering, material and spare parts support, inspection report analysis, and repair coordination
* Lead end-to-end material (spare parts) handling processes and ways of working on the frontline level in alignment with Global Spares Supply, ensuring the availability, pricing, and flow of spare parts are working smoothly for Service operations
* Define and execute the service repairs strategy in alignment with regional and global objectives
* Represent service repairs in strategic planning and decision‑making forums
* Support customer discussions when required, particularly for complex repairs, tender clarifications, or material feasibility‑related topics
* Partner with regional directors and peer leaders (e.g., Service Sales, Service Operations, Customer Service) to ensure coordinated efforts that drive profitability, productivity, and customer satisfaction
* Develop and maintain repair offering and pricing, ensuring alignment with global frameworks and local market needs. Collaborate effectively with relevant global and cross‑BL stakeholders
* Ensure compliance with commercial, technical, and offering governance policies, pricing guidelines, inspection report requirements, repair processes, and data quality standards
* Act as a key enabler for the centralized repairs tendering teams by ensuring they have the guidance, tools, and resources needed to succeed
* Advance data‑driven decision‑making through accurate reporting and performance analytics
* Support target setting and KPI follow‑up related to repair volumes, customer outcomes, and commercial performance. Lead FL repairs efficiency management and tracking, and ensure incentives are clear according to the annual process
* Lead, coach, and develop managers and team leaders to build a high‑performing, customer‑centric organization. Foster a culture of collaboration, accountability, and continuous improvement
* Lead cross‑functional projects and improvement initiatives across processes, IT tools, and performance management to strengthen repair efficiency and ways of working.

**Experience:**

* Bachelor’s degree in a related field. Master’s degree (MBA or equivalent) preferred
* Proven track record of leading teams in commercial management and service operations in a global organization
* Proven experience managing P&L or financial performance
* Strong proficiency in English and the local language

**Competencies:**

* Ability to understand technical scopes, market needs, commercial drivers, and the financial impact of repair decisions
* Able to work effectively with cross‑functional teams such as service operations, service sales, customer service, and regional leadership
* Skilled at coaching managers, supporting team development, and fostering a high‑performance, customer‑focused culture
* Strong transformational leadership capabilities, with the ability to drive change and strengthen common ways of working across repair teams.

*At KONE, we are focused on creating an innovative and collaborative working culture where we value the contribution of each individual. Employee engagement is a key focus area for us and we encourage participation and the sharing of information and ideas. Sustainability is an integral part of our culture and the daily practice. We follow ethical business practices and we seek to develop a culture of working together where co-workers trust and respect each other and good performance is recognized. In being a great place to work, we are proud to offer a range of experiences and opportunities that will help you to achieve your career and personal goals and enable you to live a healthy and balanced life.*

Read more on *https://careers.kone.com/en/*

title: Personal Assistant to Director
description:
We are looking for a highly organized and proactive Personal Manager to support the Director in managing daily operations, schedules, communications, and business activities. The ideal candidate should be detail-oriented, trustworthy, and capable of handling confidential information while ensuring smooth coordination between teams and clients.

Key Responsibilities

* Manage the Director’s daily schedule, meetings, and appointments
* Handle emails, calls, and communication on behalf of the Director
* Coordinate with team members, partners, and clients
* Assist in business planning, project coordination, and task follow-ups
* Organize travel plans, bookings, and itineraries
* Prepare reports, presentations, and documents for meetings
* Maintain confidential records and business information
* Support social media, branding, and public communications if required
* Ensure smooth execution of day-to-day business activities
* Track important deadlines, projects, and commitments

Job Types: Full-time, Permanent, Fresher

Pay: AED2,000.00 - AED4,000.00 per month

Work Location: In person

title: QA/QC Director - Construction
description:
We are hiring **QA/QC Director - Construction Background** to join our team in VICC in Dubai.

**Job Summary:**

To provide visionary and technical leadership for the Quality Assurance and Quality Control function across all projects, ensuring that construction quality consistently meets or exceeds design intent, regulatory requirements, and industry best practices. Director shall drive a culture of excellence and discipline where every project delivered under his leadership reflects top-tier workmanship, material integrity, and compliance with the company’s high standards.

**Key Responsibilities:**

* Lead the company-wide Quality Assurance and Quality Control strategy to ensure all projects meet the highest standards of workmanship, materials, and compliance.
* Establish, implement, and continuously improve the corporate Quality Management System (QMS) in line with ISO and applicable international and local construction standards.
* Ensure all project teams, zonal QA/QC managers, and engineers operate under unified QA/QC procedures and consistent quality benchmarks.
* Review, approve, and monitor the implementation of project-specific Quality Plans, Inspection & Test Plans (ITPs), Method Statements, and Material Approval Requests (MARs).
* Enforce strict adherence to approved drawings, specifications, and codes to achieve defect-free construction and first-time quality.
* Conduct regular quality audits and inspections at site and zone levels, ensuring corrective and preventive measures are implemented effectively and promptly.
* Oversee and validate quality documentation, testing protocols, and inspection reports for all trades including civil, structural, architectural, and MEP works.
* Ensure project teams proactively identify quality risks, perform root cause analysis, and implement preventive measures to avoid rework and delays.
* Drive accountability among QA/QC Managers, Engineers, and Inspectors to maintain consistent quality performance and reporting discipline across all projects.
* Evaluate and approve materials, suppliers, and subcontractors based on stringent quality and compliance criteria.
* Establish quality KPIs and dashboards to measure zone-wise and project-wise performance, reporting trends to senior management.
* Mentor and develop QA/QC staff through training, technical workshops, and on-site coaching to enhance competency and ownership.
* Lead client and consultant quality meetings to resolve complex issues and reinforce the company’s commitment to excellence.
* Initiate and drive continuous improvement programs aimed at elevating construction quality, minimizing non-conformities, and maximizing client satisfaction.
* Ensure all projects delivered by the company are recognized for superior quality standards, workmanship excellence, and zero-compromise execution.

**Qualifications and Experience:**

* Bachelor’s Degree in Civil Engineering, Construction Engineering, or related discipline (Master’s Degree in Construction Management or Quality Management preferred).
* 20 or more years of experience in Quality Assurance and Quality Control within the construction industry, including at least 4 years as a senior QA/QC management or leadership role with reputed main contractors in UAE.
* Strong understanding of high-rise, mixed-use, commercial, and residential construction quality standards.

Job Types: Full-time, Permanent

title: Program Director
description:
In a world of possibilities, pursue one with endless opportunities. Imagine Next!  
At Parsons, you can imagine a career where you thrive, work with exceptional people, and be yourself. Guided by our leadership vision of valuing people, embracing agility, and fostering growth, we cultivate an innovative culture that empowers you to achieve your full potential. Unleash your talent and redefine what’s possible.

**Job Description:**

We are looking for an **Program Director** to oversee a major program in Saudi Arabia. The Program Director shall provide leadership and guidance to a team of program directors, program managers, construction and commercial directors and managers. The program is comprised of both airside and landside projects, including terminal buildings and airport facilities. They shall be responsible for the on-budget, on-budget delivery of the program and in accordance with the contractual obligations, and plan and define program goals and lead the strategies and methodologies to accomplish them; develops in-depth knowledge of client objectives, contract terms, and corporate policies.

**What you'll be doing**

* Provides leadership, direction, and management for every phase of the project, ensuring on- schedule completion within budget and in accordance with contractual obligations.
* Acts as the company representative with Client Senior Management and other delivery partners during the project execution and the focal point of contact.
* Develops Client strategic objectives and acts promptly to resolve contentions with the Clients and Stakeholders.
* Responsible for the development of implementation plans, risk management plans, quality plans, delivery partner plans, contract templates and overall program goals.
* Responsible for the overall delivery and execution of the program including safety, quality, and Parsons staff performance.
* Negotiates changes to the scope of work with the client.
* Manages Program budgets, procurement strategies and cashflow.
* Proactive leadership and management of key senior stakeholders and their expectations.

**What you'll bring:**

* Excellent oral and written communication skills, including submissions and presentations to c-suite level.
* Proven stakeholder management skills to c-suite and national representation levels e.g., Minster level interactions.
* Proven record of accomplishment as successful Executive role in Airport and Infrastructure Construction Projects.
* Twenty (20) or more year experience in the construction industry.
* Large International Airport experience.
* Experience in Collaborative Project Management or Delivery Partnerships.
* Advanced negotiation and problem-solving skills.
* University bachelor degree in Civil Engineering or similar field required.
* Project Management or Construction Management certification or registration
* University MSc level degree desirable
* GCC experience is appreciated.

title: HSE Director
description:
In a world of possibilities, pursue one with endless opportunities. Imagine Next!

**Job Description:**

Parsons is seeking a seasoned **HSE Director** with two decades of expertise to lead health, safety, and environmental excellence for one of Saudi Arabia’s landmark **Giga-projects**. Based in Riyadh, you will be responsible for orchestrating a world-class safety culture across massive, high-complexity construction environments.

**What You Will Be Doing**

* **Strategic Leadership:** Develop and execute an overarching HSE strategy aligned with Parsons’ global standards and the specific Vision 2030 requirements of the Giga-project.
* **Program Oversight:** Direct the implementation of Environmental Management Systems (EMS) and Occupational Health & Safety (OHS) protocols across multiple project phases.
* **Stakeholder Management:** Serve as the primary HSE advisor to executive leadership, client representatives, and government regulatory bodies.
* **Incident Prevention:** Lead high-level risk assessments and root-cause analyses for complex technical challenges, ensuring "Zero Harm" targets are consistently met.
* **Team Mentorship:** Oversee a large, multidisciplinary team of HSE managers and inspectors, fostering a culture of continuous professional development.

**What We Are Looking For**

* **Experience:** Minimum of **20 years** of progressive experience in HSE management, specifically within large-scale civil infrastructure or mega-construction projects.
* **Regional Expertise:** Proven track record in the Middle East (specifically Saudi Arabia) and a deep understanding of local labor laws and environmental regulations.
* **Education:** Bachelor’s degree in Occupational Health & Safety, Environmental Science, or Engineering. Professional certifications (e.g., CSP, CMIOSH, or equivalent) are mandatory.
* **Giga-Project Pedigree:** Experience managing multi-billion dollar project portfolios with high-density labor forces.
* **Leadership Style:** A visionary leader who balances technical rigor with the emotional intelligence needed to influence diverse international teams.

title: Account Director, Luxury Sales KSA
description:
Account Director, Luxury Sales KSA

Hilton is one of the largest and fastest growing hospitality companies in the world, with more than 9,000 properties with more than 1,300,000 rooms in 120 countries and territories.

In the 105+ years since our founding, we have defined the hospitality industry and established a portfolio of 25 world-class brands, with the emergence of the Tempo brand and including our flagship Hilton Hotels & Resorts brand, which is the most recognised hotel brand in the world. We have more than 200+ million members in our award–winning customer loyalty programme, Hilton Honors

**Role Overview**

The Account Director – Luxury is responsible for driving strategic sales growth across Saudi Arabia, with a strong focus on luxury travel partners, high-value accounts, and government delegations. This role plays a critical part in strengthening Hilton Luxury Brands’ presence in the region by cultivating long-term relationships, unlocking new business opportunities, and positioning our portfolio of luxury hotels globally.

Key Responsibilities

* Develop and manage a portfolio of luxury travel trade accounts across primarily the KSA and potentially middle east, with a focus on high-potential and strategic partners
* Identify, onboard, and grow new luxury accounts in line with regional and global sales objectives
* Strengthen relationships with government entities, royal offices, and protocol departments to drive luxury delegation business internationally
* Represent Hilton Luxury Brands to key stakeholders, ensuring brand positioning aligns with the expectations of ultra-high-net-worth and VIP clientele
* Work closely with global luxury hotels to align market needs, client preferences, and commercial opportunities
* Drive production, track performance, and deliver against agreed revenue targets
* Lead and support client engagement initiatives including FAM trips, luxury events, roadshows, and international trade shows
* Collaborate cross-functionally with internal teams to ensure seamless client servicing and strong post-sale execution
* Monitor market trends, competitor activity, and emerging opportunities within the luxury travel segment

**What are we looking for?**

Key Skills & Experience

* Proven experience in luxury sales, account management, or luxury travel trade (hospitality experience preferred)
* Strong network within the Saudi luxury travel ecosystem
* Experience working with government, protocol, or high-level delegation business is a strong advantage
* Excellent relationship-building and stakeholder management skills
* Commercially driven with strong negotiation and presentation abilities
* Culturally aware, detail-oriented, and comfortable engaging at senior and VIP levels

• Ability to work independently while contributing effectively within a regional and global team • Ability and flexibility to travel.

**What Success Looks Like**

* Strong growth in luxury account production across Kingdom of Saudi Arabia
* Increased number of active, high-quality luxury travel partners
* Expanded government and delegation business for Hilton luxury hotels globally
* Trusted partnerships built on long-term value, credibility, and performance

**What will it be like to work for Hilton?**

Hilton is the leading global hospitality company, spanning the lodging sector from luxurious full-service hotels and resorts to extended-stay suites and mid-priced hotels. For more than a century, Hilton has offered business and leisure travelers the finest in accommodations, service, amenities and value. Hilton is dedicated to continuing its tradition of providing exceptional guest experiences across its global brands. Our vision “to fill the earth with the light and warmth of hospitality” unites us as a team to create remarkable hospitality experiences around the world every day. And, our amazing Team Members are at the heart of it all!

#li-sh1
